title: Observability, Capacity, Flow Control, Alarms, and SLOs · Type-System Trace: Message Flow Telemetry
summary: A dashboard shows only queue depth, creates message-ID metric labels, ignores unacknowledged work and oldest age, and responds to memory alarms by adding publishers and increasing prefetch. Planned artifact: Add and verify the message flow telemetry increment in messaging telemetry, capacity, and SLO evidence board. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Instrument publish, confirm, return, route, deliver, process, ack, retry, dead-letter, and effect phases with bounded identities and causal links.
Defend tenant topology, message size, route, rate, queue count, headers, permissions, and noisy-neighbor budgets without trusting publisher metadata.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Predict Message Flow Telemetry · Theory Message Flow Telemetry
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory messaging team handles case 2281 in a multi-tenant case event backbone with three subscriber responsibilities. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to combine backlog state, oldest age, SLO, and resource alarms into bounded operations action; show recovery after the changed failure is removed; then make the broker confirmation disappear after the message may have committed. Browser code models pure messaging decisions only. amqplib, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, external effects, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Instrument publish, confirm, return, route, deliver, process, ack, retry, dead-letter, and effect phases with bounded identities and causal links.
